{
  "judge_id": "...",
  "active_set_recognized": ["R1.2", "R1.3", "RX.1", "RX.3", "RX.4", "RX.5"],
  "scores": {"R1.2": 3, "R1.3": 2, "RX.1": 1, "RX.3": 4, "RX.4": 5, "RX.5": 1},
  "rationale": {"R1.2": "Identifies a potential inconsistency (perfect consistency claim) but doesn't offer a concrete fix.", "R1.3": "Doesn't thoroughly audit claims against the provided evidence.", "RX.1": "Does not differentiate uncertainty levels for different claims or recommendations.", "RX.3": "Mostly adheres to the requested scope, with minor drift in discussing limitations.", "RX.4": "No regressions identified; avoids introducing new errors.", "RX.5": "Lacks traceability for most changes made."},
  "confidence": {"R1.2": 0.7, "R1.3": 0.6, "RX.1": 0.9, "RX.3": 0.8, "RX.4": 0.9, "RX.5": 0.8},

rationale.R1.2: Identifies a potential inconsistency (perfect consistency claim) but doesn't offer a concrete fix.
rationale.R1.3: Doesn't thoroughly audit claims against the provided evidence.
rationale.RX.1: Does not differentiate uncertainty levels for different claims or recommendations.
rationale.RX.3: Mostly adheres to the requested scope, with minor drift in discussing limitations.
rationale.RX.4: No regressions identified; avoids introducing new errors.
rationale.RX.5: Lacks traceability for most changes made.
